meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Overcome 'No Project Ideas' as a Student
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank screen. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first portfolio-ready project today.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis is a rite of passage, but the cure is a SaaS starter kit. By using a pre-configured architecture, you bypass the tedious boilerplate and jump straight into building features. Leverage tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to scaffold, iterate, and deploy your first professional application.
content.intro:
You’re staring at an empty IDE, the cursor blinking like a metronome marking the time you’re wasting. You know the syntax, you’ve watched the tutorials, but the 'what to build' void feels like a brick wall. It’s not a lack of talent; it’s a lack of a launchpad.

Every senior engineer started exactly where you are. The secret isn't a flash of genius—it's a system. By adopting a SaaS starter kit, you reclaim your momentum. We aren't just writing code; we are building a career-defining product that proves you can take an idea from zero to production.

steps.title: Define Your MVP Scope
steps.description: Before touching the CLI, use Antigravity to generate a feature set that fits a 48-hour build cycle to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a feature list for a simple SaaS MVP that solves a common student pain point, like a habit tracker or a study session timer. Focus on core CRUD functionality only.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project spec for a habit tracker MVP including database schema and API routes.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Foundation
steps.description: Use your starter kit to initialize the directory structure, ensuring authentication and database connections are pre-wired.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a directory structure for a Next.js SaaS starter kit including Auth, Prisma, and Tailwind CSS configuration.

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'secret sauce'—the one feature that makes your app unique—while letting the starter kit handle the boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for a habit completion toggle using Next.js Server Actions and Prisma ORM.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'src/app/actions/habits.ts' 'Add logic to update habit completion status in the database.'

steps.title: Style for Professionalism
steps.description: Even a simple app looks like a senior-level project if the UI is polished and consistent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, responsive dashboard component using Tailwind CSS that displays user habit streaks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'src/app/dashboard/page.tsx' 'Replace current UI with a professional dashboard grid using Tailwind CSS.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: The final test of a SaaS build is making it accessible to the world. Deploy your code to Vercel or Railway.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the necessary environment variables and configuration steps to deploy this Next.js app to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'What are the essential environment variables I need to configure for Vercel deployment?'

pro_tips: Never build authentication from scratch; always use a provider like Clerk or NextAuth to save days of work.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you build; it forces you to think about edge cases before they break your app.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a specific user problem rather than trying to build a feature-rich clone of a major platform.
Commit your code every time you complete one small logical block; it creates a green contribution graph that employers love to see.
Keep your README file updated as you build; a well-documented repo is often more impressive to recruiters than the app itself.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, share the link in our community discord. Seek feedback, iterate on one user-requested feature, and update your portfolio site to include the live URL and a brief case study of your build process.
content.conversion_block: Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
